title: Build the public local stdio MCP server that drives qmd, the govern kernel, and ~/.teamkb in-process, exposing read (search) and write (capture, govern, promote)
close_reason: Built src/local-server.ts + src/govern.ts: self-contained in-process stdio MCP server (brain_search/status read; brain_capture/govern/transition write). brain_govern hand-wires ingest→Curator.processBatch→runExport→qmd index, daemon-free. Smoke-proven end-to-end (751bbe5).

title: Upgrade brain_search to qmd hybrid query (auto-expansion + rerank) plus an embed step in govern, for natural-language recall
description: brain_search currently runs qmd search (BM25, AND-semantics) which is brittle for natural-language queries: hyphenated/compound terms (e.g. 'in-process') tokenize to zero matches and fail the AND, so verbose questions often return nothing. qmd query (hybrid: auto-expansion + vector + LLM rerank) handles NL far better but needs qmd embed (a one-time ~333MB model download + embedding pass). Decide whether to add an embed step to brain_govern and switch brain_search to qmd query. Shared improvement (the internal brain uses the same BM25 adapter).
notes: Empirical confirmation (this session): qmd hybrid 'query' with the full NL question 'does the brain run in-process without a daemon' → 1 hit, while BM25 'search in-process' → 0. The upgrade path (switch brain_search to qmd query + add an embed step in govern) fixes the natural-language recall gap. Cost: ~333MB embedding model + one embed pass per govern.

title: Add gsb.lock.json reproducible pin and a CI smoke that runs the full chain against the pinned set
Harden installer security: npm provenance and checksums, pin qmd and ICO by SHA or tag, mint a local token, drop curl-bash
Build the one-command npx installer that builds the local brain over a folder, idempotent with --resume and --clean
Decide and implement the installer fetch model: publish the needed INTKB packages to npm versus ship a prebuilt bundle
Add an index-only no-egress mode and a loud pre-flight egress consent for the ICO compile step
Productize Governed Second Brain into a public local-first plugin, installer, and marketplace listing
description: Umbrella epic for turning the Compile-Then-Govern stack into a public, local-first Governed Second Brain that outsiders (esp. the Cowork community) can install and build their own governed, qmd://-cited brain with a tamper-evident audit trail, from inside Claude Code or Cowork. Internal brain + team mode stay untouched. Phases: A public local-MCP plugin (read+write) · B npx installer (egress consent + --index-only) · C marketplace distribution · D cross-link/thesis-fold/tracking. Plus standalone MUST-FIX beads (audit-anchor HIGH, egress mode, installer security, gsb.lock.json).
notes:
GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#1 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/1. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-qy7` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-qy7` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
Phase A landed (751bbe5): the public local-first MCP plugin is built and gate-green. Read+write tools (brain_search/status, brain_capture/govern/transition) run in-process, daemon-free; capture→govern→promote→search verified end-to-end with a qmd:// citation and a verified SHA-256 hash-chained audit (1 event, 0 breaks). Both skills pass marketplace 8-field tier (Grade B); the original dead-tool gap is closed (allowed-tools match the 5 registered tools). Beads qy7.1–qy7.4 closed. Known follow-up filed: natural-language retrieval (qmd hybrid query + embed). Next: Phase B installer.
